With SAP Cloud ERP Private, SAP removes the complexity of designing, building, and operating SAP software for customers by offering a user- or metric-based subscription that bundles software and tooling licenses, infrastructure, and technical managed services—everything needed to run an SAP solution. Read more about this here: Understanding the Distinct Scope of SAP Cloud ERP Private

To ensure a smooth transition to the cloud, SAP collaborates with each customer and partner on all design, build, and operational topics relevant to SAP Cloud ERP Private, assessing the technical feasibility of each customer’s requirements and handing them over to the business unit within SAP that is responsible for designing, building and operating SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Moving to a cloud subscription service either from an on-premise datacenter or indeed from public cloud IaaS is not a trivial task. It requires due diligence to ensure that all technical and operational requirements are met, and where deviations exist, a solution is achieved to deliver the same business outcome.

To support our customers’ transition to the private cloud, SAP offers a safeguarding process which covers all the technical and operational requirements. This process, which we call Technical Assessment, is delivered by a unit within Global Cloud Operations called Cloud Architecture & Advisory (CAA).

This document explains the role and value of the Technical Assessment process in the journey to SAP Cloud ERP Private. It outlines how SAP works with customers and partners to achieve the following:

  • Advise on suitable private cloud options and high-level architecture choices
  • Assess technical requirements and align expectations across stakeholders
  • Transition the agreed outcomes into a clear and feasible target architecture before delivery

By providing an overview of the assessment domains, decision points, and outcomes, this document aims to give stakeholders a clear understanding of how the Technical Assessment helps ensure a secure, scalable, and successful transition to SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Figure1 : Technical Assessment Flywheel

Advise the customer on the private cloud offerings within the SAP GCO portfolio

A key factor to ensure a successful transition to SAP’s Private Cloud is to understand the private cloud offering and the details of each technical domain such as:

 

Technical Domain

Expected customer’s stakeholder

SAP Cloud ERP Private Overview

All key stakeholders

Cloud Technical Architecture

Infrastructure, application, basis, and network stakeholders

Network and Integration

Infrastructure, network, and security stakeholders

Cybersecurity and Compliance

Security, network, infrastructure, application, and basis stakeholders

Cloud Managed Services and Operating Model

All key stakeholders

Customer Onboarding

Infrastructure, application, basis, and network stakeholders

Table 1: Technical Domains and expected participants

These domains provide a structured lens to assess customer needs and requirements as well as position the right GCO services. Depending on the complexity of the requirements, some topics will require deep‑dive workshops to reach a shared understanding and robust solution options. This collaborative approach enables tailored architectures and operating models that align to the customer’s objectives and proactively address potential challenges during the move to SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Assess the technical feasibility of the customer’s requirements

Figure2: Technical Assessment aspects

Aligning the customer’s current and target (“to‑be”) state is essential to define a clear path for their cloud journey. This assessment ensures both sides share the same expectations on scope, timelines, and responsibilities.

Throughout this process, SAP validates that the customer’s business and operational requirements can be implemented within SAP’s private cloud delivery standards. It also confirms that the solution follows GCO best practices for business continuity, operations, and cybersecurity.

Some aspects to be covered during the assessment are the following:

Aspect

Topics to be discussed

Decisions to be taken

Deployment option, platform, and region

As described in the blog From On-Premise to Cloud: Making the Right SAP ERP Move, SAP offers several deployment options for SAP Cloud ERP Private, such as hyperscaler, SAP data center, customer data center, and premium suppliers – each available on different platforms and in multiple regions. It is the customer’s responsibility to choose the deployment model and select the platform and target regions, with support from the SAP CAA team to align with requirements, synergies, and the customer’s cloud strategy.

  • Deployment option (hyperscaler, SAP data center, customer data center, premium supplier)
  • Cloud provider or hyperscaler for the deployment
  • Regions (main and disaster recovery)

Network and communication infrastructure

Each platform provides different private and internet-facing connectivity options. Based on the chosen deployment option, platform, region, and performance needs, the customer must decide how to connect to SAP Cloud ERP Private, including any requirements for network segregation and DNS integration that should be clarified during the Technical Assessment.

  • Connectivity options for private and public access
  • Bandwidth required
  • DNS integration method (zone transfer, conditional forwarding, etc.)
  • Load balancing requirements

Application platform and integrations

It’s crucial to clarify which bill of materials, applications, and solutions are in scope for the private cloud, how they are offered in the GCO portfolio (for example, high availability, disaster recovery, and SLAs), and how they will integrate with SAP and non‑SAP systems.

  • Bill of material and solution’s versions
  • Business continuity options and SLAs
  • Technical elements required for integration with SAP and non-SAP systems

Solution sizing

Solution sizing from the customer or partner must be translated into cloud technical sizing (for example, database and storage), since accurate sizing is key for a smooth transition and optimal performance.The blog Sizing Your Success: A Guide to SAP Cloud ERP Private is a recommended reference for this activity.

  • Target sizing of the different solutions and tiers
  • Expected growth during the contract’s runtime

Security and regulatory requirements

As explained in the blog RISE with SAP: Comparing the Security of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition Vs SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, SAP applies a multilayered security strategy and a zero‑trust approach across its cloud services, with controls such as encryption, access control, auditing, compliance, logging, monitoring, and secure operations governed by SAP standards and policies. This underpins the SAP Cloud ERP Private security and regulatory framework. Each organization also has its own security and regulatory needs, and where specific requirements go beyond the standard framework, SAP reviews them and designs appropriate measures.

  • Confirm that SAP’s security framework meets the customer’s requirement in terms of cybersecurity and regulation.
  • Identify any special requirement and find a solution for it.

Best practices and operational model

SAP Cloud ERP Private provides several operating models and SLA levels to suit different customer sizes and requirements. This includes defining backup expectations and reviewing roles and responsibilities (for example, via a RACI matrix) to identify and close any gaps.

  • Operational model
  • Availability SLAs for solutions
  • Customer’s specific backup SLA
  • Review of roles and responsibilities RACI matrix to identify potential gaps

Table 2: Technical aspects to be assessed

The outcome of the technical domain sessions and the customer landscape assessment is a clear and accurate “to‑be” picture of the customer environment, including the transformation strategy. This serves as the blueprint to design and build the final landscape in SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Transition the customer’s requirements to GCO for delivery

SAP Cloud ERP Private is a managed private cloud service delivered by Global Cloud Operations (GCO), requiring a structured handover of customer requirements to SAP.

Early transition planning: Although the formal transition begins after the Cloud ERP Private agreement is signed, preparation should start early to avoid deployment delays that could jeopardize the project timeline.

Support process: The Cloud Architect and Advisory (CAA) team guides customers and partners through this process by collecting onboarding information, coordinating milestones, and ensuring smooth delivery to SAP teams.

The following outlines typical onboarding information to be collected:

Type of Input

Input Needed

Operational


 

  • Support user (S-user)
    Type of deployment (greenfield or brownfield)
  • Planned downtime
    SAP Best Practices activation
  • Financial relevance
  • Customer contact information
  • Regulatory requirements – GxP, HIPPA, data sovereignty
  • Cybersecurity requirements

SAP-specific


 

  • System parameters (SIDs, clients, languages, etc.)
  • Target versions

Network and DNS

  • Connectivity method
  • Connectivity data
  • Private IP addresses ranges
  • DNS information

Table 3: Onboarding information

Outcome of Technical Assessment: baseline and working document

The Technical Assessment process produces the Technical Assessment Report, summarizing all sessions and workshops, the proposed to‑be architecture for SAP Cloud ERP Private, and any assumptions or exceptions from discussions.

This report is shared with the customer for review and sign-off. It serves as the baseline for the commercial contract, delivery onboarding and is a living document throughout the contract lifecycle.

Innovate and grow in SAP Cloud ERP Private

Everything described above represents continuous engagement throughout the entire contract lifecycle, not a one-time effort.

For new customer requirements, the GCO team supports the customer and partner through a renewed technical assessment process. Depending on the requirement, relevant assessment domains and onboarding details will vary, but GCO provides full guidance—advising on goals and value drivers, identifying innovation opportunities, and extending the Cloud ERP Private scope via the change request process.

entry.title: SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, 2025 FPS01 – What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, 2025 FPS01. With this release, we further expand the Predictive Labor Demand Planning capabilities available for SAP EWM, add in more functionalities and scenarios for the Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration, deliver a host of extensibility and user experience improvements, strengthen our Suite Integration capabilities, and much more.

And unlike many of our past product release blog posts, I would like to begin today’s overview with the latest updates to our AI capabilities in Extended Warehouse Management. To find out more technical details and information on each feature (such as Customization steps) I shall link the corresponding “What’s New” viewer from the help portal in the title of the subchapters.

ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE

With our AI strategy for Logistics and warehousing, we aim to address further on the labor and resource shortage in the warehouse execution. We achieve this via machine learning–based predictive analytics to forecast current and future workloads and conduct forecast based on long-term historical data across warehouse operations, as well as via Joule copilot to provide advanced warehouse operation, and proposing insightful solutions to streamline the outbound process. With this feature pack release, we continue to advance towards this goal via the following updates.

Feature enrichment for AI-assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning

In the previous two product releases, our teams have delivered a solid foundation for Predictive Labor Demand Planning (LDP), through which warehouse operators and shift managers can:

  • Predict planned workload on Outbound Delivery Order and Warehouse Order/Warehouse Task level based on historical data collected from RF Picking and Packing activities
  • Use a minimum of 2 weeks of historical data and predicts the short-term labor demand in next days or weeks
  • Utilize the SAP Smart Business dashboards to gain insights on workload duration, calculated Full-time-equivalents (FTE) and other important aspects such as volume, weight, and number of expected items

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01 release, the solution now supports the persistence of aggregated historical workload data and determines planned duration for outbound delivery staging.

Additionally, we have expanded the range of executed picking workload that can be captured – such as the start date and time - to improve LDP reporting, KPI calculation, and historical workload tracking for warehouse task confirmations via the external API. This has been done by integrating and enabling a two-step pick process for historical records and time tracking using an existing remote-enabled ODATA-API: API_WAREHOUSE_ORDER_TASK_2.

Also, on the topic of reporting for Labor Demand Planning, we now directly integrate the pLDP statistics to the Fiori App “Warehouse KPIs”, providing users with a view of workload forecast KPIs directly as configurable cards, alongside seamless navigation to the detailed smart-business drill-down apps.

The user experience is further enhanced through dynamic date functions. The first forecast graph now displays the total forecast value with drill-down to activity areas for deeper insight. Workload forecasts can be analyzed through five KPI measures—Duration, Volume, Weight, Capacity Consumption, and Number of Items—offering a clear and structured view of operational demand.

Joule integration with the EWM Outbound process

In the upcoming releases of Joule monthly schedule, we will release the full capabilities of Joule feature “Talk to your Warehouse Outbound process”, providing users with the possibility not only to retrieve data from EWM backend on Outbound Delivery Orders, but also to trigger next steps and accelerate the outbound fulfillment process. In addition, users can also navigate directly in the Joule conversation to the corresponding FIORI apps.

Get an overview on all available Joule capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition via our dedicated SAP Help – Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - highlighting transactional and informational capabilities to stay up-to-date.

ADVANCED SHIPPING & RECEIVING

Integration between transportation and warehouse logistics is a significant contributor towards logistics efficiency, synchronizing process changes between warehouse and transportation, reducing overall logistics-costs by optimizing and streamlining freight planning with warehouse execution. To further tighten this integration, with this release we provide the following enhancements and updates to Advanced Shipping & Receiving.

Perform ad-hoc loading

In this latest release, we further streamline the loading process by enabling warehouse operators to assign Outbound Delivery Orders to freight orders during the radio frequency (RF) loading process in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. When the first handling unit (HU) is scanned and loaded, the complete Outbound Delivery Order, including the remaining HUs, is assigned to the Freight Order. Ad-hoc loading can be performed in the following RF transactions:

  • Loading By HU
  • Loading By Door
  • Loading By Bill of Lading

This new process helps to simplify loading in a busy warehouse, in such scenarios as when:

  • There are regular freight orders to a certain plant or region. When the truck arrives, all the HUs in the staging area for this plant or region can be loaded onto it. This procedure is typically used in combination with the immediate loading process.
  • A truck at the door has some space left that can be further filled. Additional suitable HUs can be loaded onto the truck, and the system automatically assigns the corresponding Outbound Delivery Order to the Freight Order.

To see if an Outbound Delivery Order is relevant for Ad-Hoc Loading, users can check in the Outbound Delivery Orders app (transaction /SCWM/PRDO) and/or the Warehouse Monitor app (transaction /SCWM/MON).

Warehouse-Driven Outbound Delivery Order split

In the shipping process, sometimes companies need to split Handling Units based on actual pick, pack and stage status, according to various exceptions in the outbound process. To increase warehouse and shipping flexibility and allow the adjustment of deliveries according to operational reasons, we provide the option to do a warehouse-driven Outbound Delivery Order split with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition FPS01.

With this feature, warehouse and shipping operators can split Outbound Delivery Orders in the warehouse-driven scenario of Advanced Shipping and Receiving triggered from EWM. For example, for an urgent customer order and in case of stock insufficiency, it is now possible to split the Outbound Delivery Order into available quantity and the remaining quantity. So the available quantity can be sent to the customer as planned, and the remaining quantity can be sent at a late point in time, while the corresponding new split-Freight Unit can be considered for further planning in Transportation Management.

 

The split of Outbound Delivery Orders can take place up until the status Ready for Shipping is set. The Outbound Delivery Orders can be split in the following ways:

  • Split Outbound Delivery Order item into a new Outbound Delivery Order – the unpicked (and unpacked) item quantity is split from an Outbound Delivery Order into a new Outbound Delivery Order. For example, there is currently not enough quantity available in the warehouse, but the quantity that has already been picked could be shipped with an available transportation vehicle. The picked quantities stay in the original Outbound Delivery Order, and the unpicked quantities move to a new Outbound Delivery Order, including the corresponding Freight Unit in Transportation Management (TM).
  • Split handling unit (HU) into new Outbound Delivery Order – one or more selected HUs are split into a new Outbound Delivery Order. For example, an HU is to be delivered on a different date in the future. The HU, which is split, is moved to a new Outbound Delivery Order, while the remaining HUs in the original Outbound Delivery Order are kept in that Outbound Delivery Order. This scenario works in classic Advanced Shipping and Receiving loading as well as Immediate Loading.
  • Split remaining parts into new Outbound Delivery Order – the HUs in the Outbound Delivery Order that are to be delivered are selected, and the system splits the remaining items or HUs into a new Outbound Delivery Order. The selected HUs are kept in the original Outbound Delivery Order. For example, the HUs for an Outbound Delivery Order which are already fully picked, packed, and loaded to a specific transportation vehicle. However, the remaining parts of the Outbound Delivery Order will not be ready for picking, packing, or loading in time. The remaining parts are split to another Outbound Delivery Order and the loaded HUs are sent to the customer.

The setting for this functionality can be found in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Goods Issue Process > Outbound Delivery > Define Document Types for Outbound Delivery Process.  To enable splitting of Outbound Delivery Orders, Outbound Delivery Order Split must be chosen in the Outbound Split setting for the combination of document type and document category.

Harmonized SAP Global Trade Service integration

A final highlight I would like to mention in Advanced Shipping & Receiving is that the integration pattern for ASR and SAP Global Trade Services (SAP GTS) is now available for Extended Warehouse Management embedded in SAP S/4HANA. This integration pattern is harmonized with the integration pattern used in the decentralized setup for the Advanced Shipping and Receiving process (which is available since February 2025 with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release) and features enhance stability and reliability.

To enable the new integration pattern, users must ensure that the status type “DSC - LE Distribution Confirmation” is active in all relevant outbound delivery status profiles, in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Cross-Process Settings > Delivery - Warehouse Request > Status Management > Define Status Profiles.

SUITE INTEGRATION

Integration is a core pillar of our investments, by which we provide a deep and native integration with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud solution components, along with other SAP cloud solution offerings for seamless integration of comprehensive logistics end-to-end processes incl. production integration, customs processing and more. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 2025, FPS1 we deliver the following enhancements.

nJIT Supply-to-Customer (S2C)

Just-in-Time (JIT) processing is a common practice in manufacturing industries for efficient demand-driven production and logistics across supply chains. Just-In-Time Supply to Customer covers JIT processes from the perspective of a supplier. A JIT supply-to-customer(S2C) call is a request of the customer to deliver components to a ship-to location, specifying the requested quantity, date, time, and destination within the ship-to location for each item. The JIT S2C supply starts with the receipt of JIT calls from the customer. These JIT calls are directly relevant for shipping, assuming there is stock available from in-house production or external procurement. The shipping process starts with delivery creation and ends with posting goods issue. After goods issue, the settlement is done from creating the billing document until receiving the payment.

With the current release, we enable production planners, warehouse operators and shipping specialists to work with the S2C process for Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA in their storage locations. After the production and packing of the sequenced component groups, the system creates outbound deliveries using the Manage Customer JIT Calls by Component Groups app, including handling unit numbers from packing. These deliveries are then transferred to EWM. In EWM, the system creates an Outbound Delivery Order that contains the lowest-level HU details (to be used as pick-HUs) per delivery item. The HU warehouse tasks can be created manually or automatically to pick highest-level HUs and move them to the staging area. If HUs are already staged, the system automatically creates and confirms the corresponding pick tasks, reducing manual steps.

Quantity Correction Process in Advanced Returns Management (ARM)

There are cases when after material inspection is done for received customer returns, there rises a need to correct received quantities, for example when not the entire quantity was returned, or if upon inspection of the goods, some are beyond recovery and must be sent to salvage rather than storage.

With this release we enable logistical follow-up action “0023 - Goods Receipt Quantity Correction” to correct the goods receipt quantity after inspection so that discrepancies between the returned quantity and inspected quantity can be accurately reflected in the system. The outbound delivery is directly sent to Extended Warehouse Management with a corresponding document type and item type. Automatic warehouse task creation and confirmation to a staging zone, including an automatic goods issue posting is performed to enable the stock correction

To run the quantity correction process in Advanced Returns Management, users must activate business configuration set (BC set) Outbound Delivery Process for ARM Quantity Correction (/SCWM/DLV_OUTBOUND_ARM_QC). There is a proposed mapping for Logistics Execution (LE) document and item types to EWM document and item types in the BC set documentation.

Quality Inspections for Products in Outbound Delivery Orders

A final highlight worth spotlighting in the area of Suite Integration is the integration of quality management to outbound warehouse processes. With this integration, warehouse operators and quality inspectors can make sure that specific qualities of a product (for example, paperwork completion) are checked before the relevant Outbound Delivery Order leaves your warehouse. To this end, we introduced a new indicator on the EWM Outbound Delivery Order item – Relevant for Q-Inspection in Outbound.

With this indicator, the system checks the status of the quality inspection at goods issue posting in EWM and prevent successful posting in case quality inspection is not completed. The check prevents goods issue posting for EWM Outbound Delivery Orders with open inspection lots with origin:

  • 10 - Delivery to Customer with Sales Order
  • 11 - Delivery to Customer Without Sales Order
  • 12 - General Delivery

To use this feature, IOT 8 must be activated in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Cross-Process Settings > Quality Management > Basics and Integration > Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs.

CONTINUOUS MODERNIZATION & SIMPLIFICATION

Our commitment to modernization and continuous improvements not only enhances functionality but also provides users with a seamless and intuitive interaction, helping businesses drive efficiency, optimize processes, and deliver superior experiences to their stakeholders. To that end, I would like to highlight the following features from this latest release.

Use Stock Transfer Orders with Valuated Stock in Transit (SIT)

Starting with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 2025 FPS01 release, we enable the use of EWM-managed storage locations as receiving locations in the stock transfer process with valuated SIT. Valuated SIT can be used in both intra-company processes and processes that move stock between companies.

Quantify changes are not supported in the scenario, besides that an inbound delivery item with valuated SIT can be set to zero in a stock transfer order process, which cancels the delivery. Subsequently, the quantity of an inbound delivery item with valuated SIT cannot be changed to anything other than zero in a stock transfer order process.

The field EWMStockTransferOrderType is now also available in the OData API Warehouse Inbound Delivery - Read, Update (A2X) (API_WHSE_INB_DELIVERY_2). To enable this feature, the Prev. Qty Upd STO checkbox must be selected in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Goods Receipt Process > Inbound Delivery > Define Item Types for Inbound Delivery Process.

Specify Handling Unit Number for Component Consumption

In certain production environments, the Production Supply Areas (PSA) are managed by the Handling Units. Before this release, production warehouse workers had to consume the entire component group HU at the PSA before you can register the consumption in the system. However, there are cases when only select sub-HUs are required from the PSA. In order to keep the physical world and the system in sync, we now allow users to specify which HU the component is to be consumed from. This strengthened integration is supported for synchronous goods movement postings via the Production Order Confirmation API (API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV), which passes the handling unit (HU) number for component consumption with synchronous goods movements.

CLEAN CORE & EXTENSIBILITY

The Clean Core Extensibility strategy aims to provide a modern and powerful integration option for customers and partners. Alongside supporting this strategy, we continuously adapt solution content to modernize and simplify the user experience. By regularly updating and refining Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, we ensure we remain relevant and effective in meeting current needs.

In the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release we continue to deliver on this with the following highlights:

  • Update for the Warehouse Order and Task (A2X) API – We have enhanced the existing ODATA-service Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), with the new ‘Lock/Unlock Warehouse Order’ actions on the Warehouse Order. This was done to enable efficient and lean integration of warehouse automation (e.g. robotics).
  • Enhancement of Stock List in the Warehouse Task Apps – We enabled warehouse operators to add their own fields to the stock list at warehouse task creation for warehouse request. For this, they can use the Business Add-In (BAdI) Enhancement for Stock List for Warehouse Task Creation (/SCWM/EX_CORE_CR_STOCK_LIST) to add additional fields to the display of stock at warehouse task creation for warehouse requests in the following apps:
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Inbound Delivery
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Outbound Delivery Order
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Posting Change
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Stock Transfer

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above-mentioned topics as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release (such as RF improvement to allow RF receiving of mixed HUs with Serial Numbers, deletion of Inbound Deliveries in the STO scenario, and more) I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2025, Private Edition, FPS01” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: What’s New in the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 release
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 release brings a new level of intelligence to everyday business work by expanding how AI supports users across various LoBs. At the core of this update is Joule and SAP’s agentic AI framework, where specialized agents and conversational capabilities work together to guide users through tasks, surface the right information, and provide recommendations in the moment of need. Instead of navigating complex transactions or piecing together data from multiple systems, employees can rely on natural‑language interactions to move through their processes faster and with greater confidence. This release also delivers embedded AI features that automate checks, streamline validations, and support operational decisions with real‑time insights. The overview chart below brings together all key innovations delivered with the SAP Cloud ERP Private and SAP Cloud ERP 2025 FPS01 Release, highlighting how AI, Joule capabilities, and embedded intelligence are supporting users across every line of business.

Altogether, these innovations help organizations reduce manual effort, strengthen transparency, and keep their operations running more smoothly, while allowing users to focus on the work that truly drives business outcomes.

Agentic use cases

Finance:

Mastering cash flow with the Cash Management Agent*

The Cash Management Agent supports finance teams by automating tasks that usually drain time, checking bank statement completeness, validating opening balances, and preparing cash‑flow forecasts. Many organizations struggle with fragmented financial data and delayed insights, which makes it difficult to spot potential cash shortages or opportunities to use surpluses effectively. By relying on Joule and Joule Agents to run these daily checks, calculations, and summaries, teams can shift from manual reconciliation to confident, data‑driven decisions. This automation improves accuracy and frees cash managers to focus on strategic liquidity planning instead of routine monitoring. In the end, it strengthens daily cash‑position visibility and helps organizations manage their cash resources more efficiently.

Learn more by accessing the SAP Help Portal.

* This AI offering is currently in Beta and available at no cost. Pricing will be communicated once the AI offering becomes generally available. A separate subscription is required for use or testing. Learn more.

 

R&D:

Streamlined engineering changes with the change record management*

Engineering teams often lose momentum when a simple product or process change turns into a maze of documentation, reviews, and approvals. Coordinators and engineers must manually create change records and shepherd them through the process, which can delay decisions and slow time‑to‑market. The Change Record Management Agent uses SAP Joule to assist with creating complete and consistent change records and orchestrating the necessary workflow steps. By reducing manual effort and guiding teams through the process, it helps engineering organizations keep changes moving without administrative bottlenecks. This leads to a faster, more efficient change‑management cycle and ultimately supports quicker delivery of updates and innovations.


 Use our SAP Help page to learn more.

 

Joule capabilities

Please note that certain Joule features described in this blog may not yet be available in your system and are planned for release as part of the upcoming Joule release cycle scheduled for March/April 2026.

Finance:

Joule for invoicing specialists to enable more-seamless navigation and transactional actions within billing documents

Invoicing specialists often spend far too much time clicking through multiple screens just to find the right convergent invoicing app or complete a billing‑related task. When workloads are high, even simple actions, like navigating to a billing document or making a small update, can slow the process and impact how quickly customers receive clear and accurate invoices. With expanded Joule capabilities, specialists can move directly to the right SAP Fiori apps and perform transactional actions through natural‑language prompts, without hunting for the correct tile or transaction. This conversational support reduces manual effort and helps teams resolve billing tasks more efficiently, leading to quicker processing and a better customer experience. Over time, this can even help reduce days sales outstanding by keeping billing workflows running smoothly and consistently.

 

AI-assisted accrual object creation

Preparing manual accruals often requires finance teams to gather fragmented information, apply judgment, and cross‑check historical patterns, all of which can be time‑consuming and inconsistent across entities. When deadlines are tight, variations in how preparers estimate amounts or select accounts can increase the risk of missed accruals or adjustments later in the audit process. With AI‑assisted accrual creation in Joule, users can describe their scenario in natural language and instantly receive a proposed accrual object, including category, amount, life term, and relevant GL accounts. They can refine any attribute, and Joule adapts its suggestions in real time based on the edits, gradually improving proposal quality through continued use. This guided, conversational approach streamlines preparation, increases consistency, and helps finance teams create more accurate accruals with far less effort.

 

 

Sales:

Staying ahead with AI-assisted detecting of expiring prices using Joule 

Pricing specialists often spend valuable time hunting through condition records to find prices that are about to expire, a task that becomes even more demanding when large volumes of data and tight sales timelines are involved. Missed expirations can lead to outdated pricing in customer quotes, last‑minute firefighting, and unnecessary manual corrections. With Joule detecting expiring prices, displaying relevant condition records, and even supporting validity extensions and mass changes, specialists can handle follow‑up activities with far less effort. This reduces repetitive work, minimizes human error, and keeps pricing information accurate and up to date across the system. Ultimately, it enables teams to manage pricing proactively, saving both time and operational cost.

 

 

Use our SAP Help page to learn more.

 

AI-assisted fetching and listing of open sales documents

Internal sales reps often need to scan through multiple criteria in VA05 just to find the right open sales orders, which can slow down workflows when customers are waiting for updates. Searching by document type, dates, materials, or organizational data requires navigating several fields and filters, making the process repetitive and time‑consuming. With AI‑assisted retrieval in Joule, reps can simply ask for open sales documents, and the system instantly displays orders filtered by attributes such as customer, material, creation date, or sales area. This conversational approach reduces the manual effort needed to locate the right information and helps reps respond faster and more accurately. The result is a more efficient sales process and smoother daily operations for the team.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

AI-assisted sales quotation changes using Joule 

Updating sales quotations can be surprisingly time‑consuming, especially when internal sales reps need to adjust quantities, refine pricing, change delivery dates, or capture customer‑specific references across multiple line items. These routine updates often involve navigating several fields in VA22, increasing the chance of small mistakes that can slow down the sales cycle. With AI‑assisted quotation changes in Joule, reps can make these adjustments through simple natural‑language requests, whether they need to update a quantity, modify a price via condition type, set delivery dates, or record reasons for rejection. This conversational flow speeds up the process and reduces manual effort, helping teams respond to customer requests more quickly and accurately. The result is a smoother quotation‑management experience that keeps sales moving with less friction.

 

Service:

Searching and navigating to service contracts with Joule

Service agents often spend more time searching for the right service contract than actually solving the customer’s issue, especially when navigating by status, dates, or complex contract structures. These delays can disrupt workflows and make it harder to move quickly from one task to the next. With Joule assisting through natural‑language prompts, agents can instantly search for service contracts, open details, complete releases, or jump directly to the relevant SAP Fiori functionality. This reduces unnecessary clicks and helps users focus on completing service‑related tasks instead of trying to find them. As a result, teams can deliver faster responses and manage service contracts with more confidence and less effort.

 

 

Joule for transferring in-house service objects

Transferring an in‑house service object between plants or storage locations often requires navigating several steps in the system, from creating the outbound delivery to recording the inbound receipt. These tasks can slow down technicians and coordinators who simply want to keep service operations moving. With Joule guiding the process through conversational prompts, users can initiate the transfer, add the target plant and storage location, and create the necessary deliveries without searching for the right transaction. This reduces friction in daily logistics and helps teams complete handovers more reliably. The conversational flow not only speeds up execution but also makes the transfer process easier for occasional users who don’t perform it every day.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

 

Supply Chain:

Enabling Joule to drive operational efficiency in outbound processes in SAP Extended Warehouse Management

Warehouse teams often juggle multiple screens just to track outbound deliveries, check warehouse tasks, or confirm the status of orders—slowing down operations in environments where every minute counts. As outbound volumes grow and customer expectations tighten, the effort required to manually search, summarize, and update records can become a real bottleneck. With Joule integrated into SAP EWM, users can search for outbound delivery orders, review warehouse tasks, or even create and confirm warehouse orders simply by asking in natural language. This gives frontline teams instant insights and reduces the time spent navigating complex menus or transactions. The result is faster decision‑making, smoother outbound processing, and a more intuitive experience that helps warehouses keep goods moving efficiently.

 To learn more, access our SAP Help Portal.

 

R&D:

Project assistant capabilities for the Joule copilot: read cost and revenue details of project objects

Project controllers and R&D teams often spend a lot of time gathering cost and revenue details from multiple reports, especially when they need to compare actuals, planned values, commitments, or fiscal‑year breakdowns. Navigating through different project objects, whether a project, WBS element, network, or network activity, can slow decision‑making and make routine checks feel heavier than they need to be. With Joule acting as a project assistant, users can simply ask for the financial picture of any project object and instantly receive details such as actual cost, planned cost, commitments, or revenue, including breakdowns by fiscal year, cost component, or value category. This conversational access removes the need to open complex reports or consolidate information manually, giving teams a clearer view of project performance in seconds. The result is faster insights, easier financial monitoring, and a more intuitive way for project stakeholders to stay on top of their work.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

 

Streamlining batch master insights and navigation with Joule

Reviewing batch master data often requires users to navigate across multiple fields and apps to check details such as material, plant, batch status, manufacturing date, shelf-life expiry date, or supplier, which can slow decision-making and increase the risk of missed insights under tight timelines. With Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, users can describe their requirement in natural language and instantly retrieve relevant batch master information, then navigate directly to the Manage Batches object page for deeper analysis, updates, or next actions. This conversational approach streamlines access to batch insights, enhances user experience, and improves operational efficiency through faster, more intuitive interaction.

 

Embedded AI

Finance:

AI-assisted fixed asset key figures explanation

Fixed asset reporting often raises questions that require deep accounting knowledge, especially when values are influenced by midyear acquisitions, depreciation keys, or complex calculation rules. In many organizations, answering these questions means digging through configuration tables or asking specialists to explain how a specific number was derived. With AI‑assisted explanations in Joule, users can simply request a breakdown of any fixed asset key figure and immediately see where the values come from and how they were calculated. This natural‑language guidance helps preparers and reviewers understand depreciation logic and valuation outcomes without manual analysis, reducing the risk of errors or compliance issues. As a result, teams can resolve fixed‑asset questions more quickly and consistently, with far less effort during period close or audits.

 

 

AI-assisted settlement rule proposal for asset capitalization  

Creating settlement rules for investment measures can be a painstaking task, especially when projects or internal orders need to settle to fixed assets and no automatic guidance is available. Finance teams often spend time determining the right receivers and percentages, increasing the risk of inconsistencies across measures or late adjustments when assets are capitalized. With AI‑assisted settlement rule proposals in Joule, users receive an initial suggestion for full settlement to fixed assets and can easily refine the proposal by adjusting percentages or selecting different receivers. This conversational support reduces manual effort and helps ensure more accurate asset class determination for final capitalization. The result is a smoother, more reliable settlement process that supports finance teams without adding complexity.

 

 

Supply Chain:

AI-assisted goods receipt analysis for bordero documents 

Goods receipt processing for bordero documents can be slow and error‑prone, especially when teams need to manually review carrier paperwork, create corresponding freight orders, and verify that all information matches what’s in the system. These manual checks often lead to delays at the gate, longer truck turnaround times, and unnecessary waiting for logistics partners. With SAP Document AI analyzing bordero documents, the system can automatically extract key details, propose a freight order, and highlight discrepancies between the uploaded document and existing freight data. This reduces the time spent on validations, improves accuracy, and helps teams process inbound goods more efficiently. The smoother flow not only enhances operational performance but can also reduce yard congestion by keeping vehicles moving.

 

Summary

As you look across the innovations in this release, a clear pattern emerges: AI is no longer something users “go to”, it’s something that quietly supports them throughout their day. Whether it’s a cash manager starting the morning with a clear liquidity picture, a service agent navigating directly to the right contract, or a warehouse coordinator checking outbound tasks with a simple question, Joule and embedded AI make these moments feel more connected and intuitive. These capabilities reduce the friction of everyday work, giving teams more room to focus on decisions, collaboration, and the tasks that genuinely move the business forward. And while this release delivers meaningful improvements on its own, it also lays the foundation for even more intelligent assistance, with agents and AI features becoming part of the natural rhythm of how people use SAP Cloud ERP. It’s a step toward a future where systems feel less like tools and more like partners, helping organizations work smarter, not harder. 

Thank you for taking the time to explore the latest updates of AI‑driven capabilities in SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition with us. If you’d like to stay informed about upcoming features, deep dives, and enablement content, make sure to follow updates tagged with PSCC_Enablement in the SAP Community. You’re also welcome to connect with me (@Yannick_PTT) both in the community and on LinkedIn for more insights and continuous updates.

For a deeper look into the intelligent capabilities delivered in this release, you can explore the full documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

More information

  • What’s New Viewer
  • From Innovation to Impact: SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 Release (SAP News Center)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Community
  • Help Portal SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • What's New for Joule Capabilities
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Business Suite Product Expert Training H1 2026

We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Join the Updated SAP Defense & Security Courses – Now Live!
entry.content:
Dear learners,

I’m pleased to share that the updated versions of IDS20_2601(61) and IDS30_2601(61) are now live and ready for learners. These new releases include fully corrected and revalidated hands-on exercises on the S4U SAP S/4HANA 2025 demo system, with master data and configuration aligned to the 2025 tenant.

These improvements ensure an even smoother, more reliable learning experience—perfect for anyone working with SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security scenarios.

Why Join These Courses?

Each course has been refined to match real-world Defense & Security processes and provides hands-on, scenario-driven learning. Here's a short overview:

IDS10 – Exploring Fundamentals of SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

A high-level introduction offering conceptual grounding in SAP Defense & Security, ideal for learners looking to understand core capabilities, system navigation, and foundational processes.
➡️ Great for newcomers and those planning to progress into deeper topics. 

IDS20 – Exploring Organizational Flexibility in SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

Designed for consultants and solution architects, this course dives into capability planning, organizational integration, HR topics, and accounting within the SAP D&S solution—supported by demonstrations and exercises.
➡️ Ideal for those involved in implementing or supporting Defense & Security structures.

IDS30 – Exploring Logistics and Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

A hands-on course dedicated to logistics, maintenance processes, and operational readiness in Defense & Security environments.
➡️ Perfect for practitioners working with material management, maintenance workflows, or operational logistics.

Start Learning Today!

Access on learning.sap.com (LSC): 

  • Defense and Security learning page
  • IDS10 - Exploring Fundamentals of SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security
  • IDS20 - Exploring Organizational Flexibility in SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security
  • IDS30 - Exploring Logistics and Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

Classroom / ILT Registration:

  • IDS10 – https://training.sap.com/course/ids10
  • IDS20 – https://training.sap.com/course/ids20

Thanks,
Greg

entry.title: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: AI-assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning
entry.content:
As many of our EWM customers are excited to learn what AI capabilities we are working on, with S/4HANA Cloud for EWM, Private Edition, we’ve decided to write this blog to provide an overview on the AI-assisted predictive labor demand planning topic.

We hope it will give you good information on the released features, as well as hands-on information on how to start using it.

Feature scope of AI-assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning (pLDP): 

  • 2023 FPS03 (release date Feb. 2025), we released the backend foundation for the pLDP in which users can get planned workload based on executed workload of RF based Picking and Packing activities in the warehouse outbound processes.
  • 2025 FPS00 (release date Oct. 2026), we introduced the Machine Learning algorithm in the EWM side to learn from the historical executed workload to derive future workload forecast. Meanwhile, we have built up a new UI on the Fiori based SAP Smart Business application to show the workload forecast with drill down capabilities.
  • 2025 FPS01 (release date Feb. 2026), we have just released the following feature enrichments in the pLDP:
    • RF-based Staging is also included in the forecast.
    • API for picking can be used for executed workload recording.
    • An introduction of aggregated historical workload data, to provide long-term forecasts for future releases.
    • The forecast cards are directly integrated into the FIORI Warehouse KPI app, with a refined UI

Where can I find more information on the released features? 
We have recorded the feature demos for AI-assisted predictive labor demand planning on our IRIS platform: 

  • https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_c6j2rizz  
  • https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_pgw47fje  
  • https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_hjlcr9hf  

Additionally, we recommend that you read our SAP Help documentation and Release Information Note for pLDP - SAP Note 3577311 

How can I configure the new Predictive Labor Demand Planning? 
Great question! Last year, we hosted a Meet the Expert session detailing how to activate the new feature. To view the recording here; please register with your company email. 
We've made a simple setup: just activate it in customizing and in the ISLM (Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management) FIORI app for your machine learning scenario. The EWM system automatically learns from then on, so no extra data training is required.

Do I need to use Labor Management (LM) to use the new Predictive Labor Demand Planning? 
No you do not need to use LM here. In the case that no Labor Management is active in the EWM managed warehouses, you can still use the new pLDP.  
Of course, using LM is compatible with the new pLDP. For example, if you maintain the ELS in Labor Management, the ELS will take over here as usually this activity is quite cost-intensive, so you do not want to lose that. But in the activity areas that ELS is not maintained, you can continue to use the new pLDP for the planned workload and workload forecast.  

How flexible is the workload forecast? 
Well, as usual, we provide our customers with different BADI enhancement possibilities, for you to influence the workload calculation as well as the interpretation of the forecast data. Our goal is that you can use these for workload planning according to the specific conditions that you have in the warehouses. You might want to check the SAP Help documentation for details. 

How accurate is the new Predictive Labor Demand Planning? 
We use a triple exponential smoothing algorithm in the Predictive Analysis Library to forecast the future workload based on historical workload, and so for data accuracy, data retention of the historical workload plays a key role here. In the future, we would like to provide also a comparison based on historical workload data with past forecasts. As of now, you can check it manually in the EWM system.  
Also, for longer-term forecasts other than the next weeks, it depends on the data availability of long term aggregated historical data. We will work on that and carefully choose the right Machine Learning algorithm for upcoming future releases.  

Which processes are supported with the new LDP feature? I would like to have the same process coverage in other warehouse processes. 
As of now, it is only supported for Outbound processes, for picking, packing and staging activities. You can also use POSC (Process-Oriented-Storage-Control) together with the new pLDP features. We are aware of the customer requirements to expand the workload forecast to other EWM processes, e.g. Inbound, Production Integration, etc. We will address this matter in future releases.  
Your feedback as valued customers and partners is greatly appreciated; please feel free to inform us of any ways we can further support your warehouse operations regarding this topic. 

How do I know if EWM managed warehouses are eligible for using the new AI-assisted predictive labor demand planning in our EWM managed warehouses?  
This is also mentioned in the central SAP Note for pLDP: SAP Note 3577311  
Make sure that you fulfil the requirement mentioned in SAP Note 3642983  

We are S/4HANA Cloud for EWM Private Edition customer, which releases do I need to be able to use the new LDP feature? 
The minimal requirement for the eligible release is S/4HANA Cloud for EWM, Private Edition 2022 FPS0, and later releases. We have downported the 2023 FPS03 release scope already. For 2025 FPS0 release scope, we plan a downport which comes very soon. For the 2025 FPS01 release scope, we will check based on customer requirements to decide for the downport in the future. So, feel free to leave a comment.  

I have tried the new LDP feature and would like to provide feedback and future development requirements. How can I reach you? 
Thanks! This is exactly what we are looking for!  
You are always welcome to leave a comment in this blog post or write to us in private messages. Please leave your contact information and we will contact you shortly to discuss the details.  

Further reads on what's new in 2025 FPS01:

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-extended-warehouse-management-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition/ba-p/14336378

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS1: What's in it for the Utilities Industry?
entry.content:
The first feature pack (FPS1) of the SAP S/4HANA 2025 has just been released to customers. Below is an overview of its key innovations tailored specifically for the utilities industry:

1. Enterprise search for utilities master data
Using the Enterprise Search from Fiori Launchpad, you can now search various utilities-specific master data - such as premise, utility installation, point-of-delivery, device and device information record. Starting from the search results,  you can directly access frequently used apps and transactions for each master data entity. This intuitive approach significantly reduces search time and enhances operational efficiency. 

2. Graphical display of utilities interval data
A new Fiori app Display Utilities Time Series Values (F8929) is available for SAP S/4HANA Utilities, designed for the Meter Data Specialist (Utilities) role. This app delivered detailed insights into time series values from EDM profiles through a graphical representation that combines the values with related status information.

3. Archiving of utility installations and premises and deletion of unused device information records 
New archiving object ISU_EANL is now available to archive utility installations, including their related installation structure and points-of-delivery. Additionally, the archiving object ISU_EVBC enables the archiving of premises. Especially the first one (ISU_EANL) comprises comprehensive validations for dependent data, making sure that all related documents and master data entities are archived beforehand. Furthermore, a new report REG_DEV_INFO_REC_CLEANUP, has been introduced to delete unused device information records. 

4. More flexible completeness check for profile values
Based on customer feedback from the SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities, transaction EEDMPROFVALCHECK (Check Whether Profile Values Are Complete) has been enhanced:

  1. The meter reading unit and service type of the utility installation are now available as additional selection criteria.
  2. Existing BAdI ISU_BI_RTP_STATUS_CHECK method STATUS_CHECK_EXT now provides more precise information regarding the billability of profile values.

5. Additional CDS views for time series data

SAP S/4HANA Utilities now includes multiple CDS views for time series data extraction, supporting various interval lengths, status fields, and related attributes. For details on these new CDS views, refer to the What's New.

6. Enhancements in  SAP Market Process Management

The What's New  section in the SAP Help Portal outlines several enhancements related to SAP Market Process Management.  For full details, please refer to the following link: What's New . You can find utilities-related content in a dedicated section under Industries > Energy & Natural Resources.

Finally - here is a list of previous SAP Community blog posts, beginning with release SAP S/4HANA 2023:

 SAP S/4HANA release
(and feature pack)		Available
since		 
2023Oct 2023SAP S/4HANA 2023: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
2023 FPS1Feb 2024SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
2023 FPS2Oct 2024SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
2023 FPS3Feb 2025SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS3: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
2025Oct 2025SAP S/4HANA 2025: What's in it for the Utilities Industry?

entry.title: What is new in S/4HANA Manufacturing for Production Engineering & Operations (PEO) in 2025 FPS1
entry.content:
With the 2025 FPS1 release of SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for Production Engineering and Operations (PEO), SAP continues to strengthen its manufacturing capabilities by delivering features that boost engineering efficiency, improve shop floor flexibility, and streamline daily operations.

In this blog, I’ll walk you through the key innovations included in this feature pack – and what they mean for production engineers, supervisors, and manufacturing operations teams.

 

Production Engineering Efficiency Enhancements

🔁Alternative Sequences of Operations in Shop Floor Routings

PEO now allows production engineers to create alternative and parallel sequences directly within shop floor routings.

This is especially helpful when:

  • Work can be done on an alternative machine
  • Different inspection paths exist
  • Certain steps can be bypassed
  • Different lot-size specific process flows are required

Supervisors can switch to these sequences at the order level, giving operations more flexibility without modifying the standard routing.

 

Why it matters:

Greater process and scheduling flexibility → reduced lead time, better resource utilization, and faster response to shop floor contingencies. 

🧩 Mass Management of Components in Order-Specific Shop Floor Routings

Engineers can now manage multiple components at once—adding, editing, or replacing them in a single table-based view.

Items marked Not Editable remain protected.

Why it matters:

Huge efficiency gains during order changes and rework scenarios, where batch updates to components are common. 

🔄Manage Alternative Components Using Alternative Item Groups (AIGs)

PEO now supports creating and managing alternative item groups for order-specific shop floor routings.

You can:

  • Add, edit, or remove alternative components
  • Move components into new/existing AIGs
  • Create new AIGs for new components

Why it matters:

Allows the shop floor to use valid alternatives without touching master data — a big win for flexibility and compliance. 

🔧Flexible Rework Routings

Engineers can now create rework routings with flexible processing, enabling:

  • Sequence-independent processing
  • Cross-operation predecessor relationships

This requires the production order’s routing to have flexible processing enabled as well.

Why it matters:

Rework becomes smoother and more adaptable, reducing bottlenecks and manual coordination. 

📝  Long Texts for Externally Processed Operations

Long text descriptions can now be authored externally processed operations — and the system automatically transfers this text to:

  • The production order
  • The purchase requisition

Why it matters:

Clearer communication with suppliers + fewer manual updates = reduced errors and faster processing. 

🎨Custom Text Formats via New BAdI

A new BAdI allows companies to define custom heading formats in the rich text editor used for standard texts and work instructions.

Why it matters:

Guarantees consistent formatting aligned with corporate branding standards, without manual adjustments by engineers. 

2. Flexible Production Operations Enhancements

🔀Order Split for Serialized Products with Sequences

PEO now supports splitting shop floor orders even when serialized products use alternative or parallel sequences.

PEO automatically determines which sequences remain feasible for the new order.

Why it matters:

Significant time savings by allowing completed units to progress independently, reducing waiting time and simplifying sequencing.

 

⏸️ Action Handlers for Hold/Release Operations

Production process specialists can now configure automated or manual actions that trigger when applying or releasing a hold—for example:

  • Additional quality checks
  • Automatic follow-on system actions

 

Why it matters:

More controlled, automated, and auditable hold handling with reduced manual overhead.

 

🛠️ Additional Code Groups for Defect Recording

Supervisors can now define additional reason code groups for defect recording. Operators can then choose from any of these groups.

 

Why it matters:

More accurate defect categorization → richer insights and more effective quality improvement initiatives.

 

Wrapping Up

The 2025 FPS1 updates for SAP S/4HANA PEO focus on one overarching theme:

Empowering production engineers and shop floor teams with flexibility, speed, and clarity.

Whether it’s expanding routing capabilities, streamlining rework, improving communication with suppliers, or making shop floor execution more adaptable, these features help manufacturers operate with greater agility and precision.

If you’re already using PEO, many of these enhancements can immediately simplify daily operations. If you’re planning a transition to S/4HANA Manufacturing, these innovations highlight why PEO continues to be a strong foundation for digital manufacturing.

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Learning Map
entry.content:
The ABAP Cloud development model (ABAP Cloud) is the foundation of ABAP-based development at SAP, its partners, and customers, designed from the ground up to adhere to clean core principles. It brings together evolving technologies and methodologies into a sustainable, long-lasting programming model. 

The ABAP Cloud development model encompasses all aspects of modern cloud-ready software development, resulting in a wide range of topics that need to be understood. Therefore, clear guidance is helpful for getting started with and navigating ABAP Cloud.

Discover the ABAP Cloud Learning Map🗺️ – your guide to mastering the ABAP Cloud development model. It provides an overview of essential topics and skills, along with a curated collection of links to key resources covering the technologies, tools, approaches, and practices that drive cloud-ready, upgrade-stable ABAP development. 📚Whether you’re starting out or filling knowledge gaps, the learning map helps you navigate modern ABAP Cloud development at your own pace. 

🔗Access it here: ABAP Cloud Learning Map↗

entry.title: Where ORD Is Used in SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud & SAP Databricks
entry.content:
SAP Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is becoming a foundational standard across the SAP ecosystem because it provides a consistent, self‑describing metadata layer for APIs, events, business objects, and data products. This metadata standardization is what enables smooth interoperability across SAP’s modern data and integration platforms.

Below are the key places where ORD is used today — and how it empowers seamless integration, especially with SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Databricks.

1. SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com)

SAP Business Accelerator Hub uses ORD as one of the primary mechanisms for publishing APIs and events. Any API showing an ORD ID on its overview page is officially ORD-enabled, meaning it exposes standardized metadata that can be consumed by SAP tools and data platforms.

This ensures:

  • Consistent API descriptions
  • Discoverability by SAP’s central aggregators
  • Compatibility with downstream systems like SAP Business Data Cloud
This is the first indicator that an API can participate in the broader data product ecosystem. 
 

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP uses ORD internally to improve SAP-to-SAP integration and provide a unified metadata layer that development tools (like SAP Business Application Studio) can consume for enhanced API discovery and automation.

 

3. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

SAP Business Data Cloud actively uses ORD to describe Data Products and make them discoverable to consumers across the SAP ecosystem, including SAP Datasphere. ORD helps BDC deliver a unified catalog with:

  • Descriptions of datasets
  • Relationships
  • Capabilities
  • Higher-level metadata structures like data products and business entities
In addition, third‑party systems such as SAP Databricks are required to describe data products using ORD before publishing them into SAP Business Data Cloud.
 
This ensures smooth catalog integration and consistent metadata across SAP and non-SAP data sources.
 

4. SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere consumes data products published to SAP Business Data Cloud — and because BDC requires ORD‑based metadata, Datasphere gains:

  • Automatically discoverable data products
  • Standardized metadata for modeling
  • Compatibility with external data such as Databricks Delta Lake

Datasphere uses ORD indirectly because it relies on BDC’s ORD-driven catalog for understanding and federating data assets.

Additionally, SAP’s hybrid integration patterns show that Datasphere can integrate with Databricks using JDBC or federated models — and ORD ensures data products between these systems are consistently described.

5. SAP Databricks (Enterprise Databricks Integration)

SAP Databricks integrates deeply with SAP Business Data Cloud using BDC Connect, enabling bi-directional sharing of data products. To publish a data product from SAP Databricks to SAP Business Data Cloud:

  • The metadata must be described using ORD and CSN (Core Schema Notation)
  • The data product becomes discoverable to SAP systems (e.g., Datasphere) via BDC

Furthermore, Databricks–SAP integration uses Delta Share, JDBC, and federated models, but ORD plays the critical role of standardizing data product metadata.

This creates a smooth, unified integration path:

SAP applications → ORD-based metadata → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere & Databricks

SAP Architecture Center also confirms this hybrid pattern where business data from SAP and non‑SAP (Databricks) is harmonized through Business Data Cloud and Datasphere, enabling interoperable AI/ML and analytics scenarios.

 Refer my SAP blog

6. Data Products Across the SAP Ecosystem

ORD also provides standardized metadata for:

  • Entity Types / Business Objects
  • APIs and Events
  • Data Products 
This consistency is the key enabler for cross‑platform interoperability — especially as Data Products become central to the SAP ecosystem via Business Data Cloud and Datasphere.

Summary: How ORD Enables Smooth Integration Across SAP & Databricks

With ORD in place:

  • APIs with an ORD ID on api.sap.com can be safely consumed to build data products in SAP Business Data Cloud.
  • Business Data Cloud uses ORD metadata to catalog and serve data products to SAP Datasphere and SAP Databricks.
  • SAP Databricks uses ORD when publishing its own data products back into Business Data Cloud.
  • SAP Datasphere leverages ORD-driven metadata to discover, model, and federate data products seamlessly.

ORD is the metadata “glue” that ensures smooth integration across:

SAP BTP → Business Accelerator Hub → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere → Databricks

And the more SAP and partner solutions adopt ORD, the smoother, more automated, and more scalable integrations become across the SAP landscape.
